pi_items.metadata_value: Software Quality Assurance Analyst / Software Developer
pi_items.verified_value: Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers

pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states 'You are a QA Engineer and Developer at a SaaS company'
Analysis of setup_task.sh and verifier.py: task requires writing Python code to pass existing pytest tests (TDD/constructive), parsing log text (regex), and summarizing counts (data_aggregation).
Counted exactly 10 test functions across test_parser.py (3), test_analyzer.py (2), test_filter.py (2), test_alerter.py (2), and test_reporter.py (1) in setup_task.sh

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated subset of 11 lines from the real NASA 1995 HTTP access log, explicitly injected via setup_task.sh. The occupation claim 'Software Quality Assurance Analyst / Software Developer' contradicts the official O*NET 15-1253.00 title 'Software Quality Assurance Analysts and Testers'. All other technical parameters are correct: there are exactly 10 test functions spread across 5 test modules, the verifier explicitly enforces a 60-point minimum pass threshold, and the task correctly features regex parsing, data aggregation, and test-driven constructive programming in Python.
